model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Florida is the safer pick because the Gators own a clear full-season quality edge, ranking No. 4 in both KenPom and Torvik with elite top-10 offense and top-five defense, while Iowa grades more like a strong but clearly lower-tier top-25 team. Iowa has a credible upset path because its offense is good enough to keep pace and this is not a seed-line mismatch in underlying quality, but Florida is still better on both ends and carries the stronger efficiency margin overall. I did not find a confirmed availability issue significant enough to change that outlook, so the Gators are the more reliable choice to advance.

reasoningStep.title: Florida over Iowa on stronger two-way profile
reasoningStep.summary: Florida rates substantially better than Iowa in both major predictive systems and has the more complete offense-defense combination, while no major injury news surfaced to materially alter the matchup.
evidence: Florida: KenPom No. 4 (AdjEM 33.83, AdjO 125.6 No. 9, AdjD 91.8); Torvik No. 4 (AdjOE 126.12 No. 11, AdjDE 92.21 No. 5), record 26-7.
Iowa: KenPom No. 25 (AdjEM 22.41, AdjO 121.8 No. 31, AdjD 99.4); Torvik No. 26 (AdjOE 122.12 No. 29, AdjDE 100.16 No. 35), record 21-12.
Florida has the edge on both offense and defense, not just one side of the ball, which lowers upset risk in a Round of 32 setting.
Recent web/ESPN searches did not surface a confirmed major availability absence for either team that would justify flipping the pick.
Iowa’s credible path is offensive shot-making against a Florida team that will face a much stronger opponent than in the 1-vs-16 opener, which keeps confidence below elite levels.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: 2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: r32-south-1
Current game label: South Round of 32 1
Round: Round of 32
Resolved teams: Florida (florida) vs Iowa (iowa)
Prior committed picks: 44
